title: Neottia aestivalis
intro: Neottia aestivalis, also known as the summer lady's tresses, is a species of orchid native to North America. The health benefits of Neottia aestivalis include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include iridoids, phenolic compounds, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Neottia aestivalis is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems.
taxonomy: Neottia aestivalis is a species of orchid native to North America. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae and is further classified into the subfamily Orchidoideae and tribe Neottieae. The genus Neottia is part of the subfamily Orchidoideae, which includes about 25 genera. Neottia aestivalis is a terrestrial orchid, which means it grows on land. It is closely related to other orchids in the Neottieae tribe, such as Neottia cordata and Neottia nidus-avis.
uses: Neottia aestivalis, also known as the summer lady's tresses, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. Its roots and stems are said to contain bioactive compounds with anti-inflammatory properties. Neottia aestivalis is sometimes used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. However, further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
